claimed_reasoning: genitive because it is the fāʿil
why_wrong: a fāʿil is nominative, not genitive; the ending may be right for another reason (e.g. a preposition) but the cited governor contradicts the case

claimed_reasoning: accusative because governed by the preposition fī
why_wrong: a preposition governs the GENITIVE, never the accusative; the named governor contradicts the asserted case
a case is asserted with no governing element named; a correct ending with no justified governor is unsafe and routes to review
the ending is right (accusative) but the role/governor is wrong where the verb makes it the mafʿūl bih; a ḥāl reading mispredicts on a jāmid second object — right answer, wrong reason
